ddls:
CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model type */,
  "application_area_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application area */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the model was uploaded */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the model */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Status of the model (active or inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_models_model_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_type_id") REFERENCES model_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_models_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_models_application_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_area_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "dataset_type" TEXT /* Type of dataset (e.g., training, validation) */,
  "size_mb" REAL /* Size of the dataset in megabytes */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset was uploaded */,
  "dataset_description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being evaluated */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the model was evaluated on */,
  "precision" REAL /* Precision score of the model */,
  "recall" REAL /* Recall score of the model */,
  "f1_score" REAL /* F1 score of the model */,
  "support" INTEGER /* Support count (number of instances in the dataset) */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date the model was evaluated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data scientist, project manager) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Status of the user (active or inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the version was uploaded */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Status of the version (active or inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_versions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trainings" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training session */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being trained */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset used for training */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date the training occurred */,
  "training_duration" REAL /* Duration of the training session in hours */,
  "training_description" TEXT /* Description of the training */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trainings_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trainings_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the model type (e.g., classification, regression) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application area */,
  "app_name" TEXT /* Name of the application area */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the application area */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken by the user (e.g., upload, download, view) */,
  "resource_accessed" TEXT /* Resource accessed by the user (e.g., model, dataset) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collaborations" (
  "collaboration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being collaborated on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user collaborating */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the collaboration (e.g., owner, contributor) */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the user joined the collaboration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collaboration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaborations_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaborations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being requested */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the request */,
  "requested_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being requested */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date the request was made */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., pending, accepted, declined) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_project_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_project_requests_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_project_requests_requested_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("requested_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., data scientist, project manager) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
